programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A musical waiter, a Boris-loving singleton, and an 80s-obsessed romantic - this time First Dates serves up chaos over a spilled drink, a bit of chemistry and a few curveballs.

programme.title: Kirsties House of Craft
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp puts celebs to the test in crafting challenges. Dr Dawn Harper and Dr Ranj Singh have a paper marbling craft-off. Chris Hollins and Laura Hamilton make paper gift bags.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at The Old Times Guesthouse in Ipswich, Suffolk, run by mother and daughter team, Linda and Kelly Langdown. The BandBers rev their engines when the group try go-karting, and it soon becomes clear that Simon and Pierre have got their eyes on the winners podium.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to The Flower Pot, in Sunbury on Thames, where owner Simon Bailey and French manager Pierre Cudon offer a stylish stay with a certain je ne sais quoi. The guests follow in Henry VIIIs footsteps with a game of real tennis at Hampton Court Palace.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit is to the family run Dolphin Hotel in Exmouth, Devon, which has been a lifes work for hotelier Jo Knowles. On arrival, Pat and Nigel find a suspicious stain. Later, the guests put on their war faces for a game of paintball, and Nigel is ambushed by Simon, but refuses to surrender.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The fourth visit is to Seaton, Devon, where hosts Pat and Nigel pride themselves on their award-winning breakfast. On arrival, Kelly and Linda have a disappointing point of view and Simon and Pierre point the finger as far as the cleaning goes. That afternoon, the guests ring the changes in a town crying competition.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: With all the visits over, the BandB owners come together to confront their rivals and discover what theyve been paid. The Old Times Guest House starts proceedings and Linda and Kelly Langdown question Simon and Pierres facilities feedback.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Emma Louise and Liv host at their 527-year-old pub in Kent, where spooky, spectral tales and a chaotic bout of drag bingo and karaoke await the BandBers.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At Ami and Marks plush glampsite in Kent, the BandBers are hailing their huts. But alarm bells are ringing for one pair in their bell tent.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Saban hosts at his 700-year-old house in Maidstone, where the magnificent historic vibes arent enough to stop evening drinks turning terrifically tense...

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mark and Anne host at their Margate town house, where the atmosphere turns icy over ice cream at the beach thanks to one guest who just cant forget about his own feedback.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: After a feisty week in Kent - when some BandBers couldnt even wait until payment day to get their gripes off their chest - it is now actually payment day.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon and Henry head to north Wales where Henry finds an antique cannon in antique dealers Sue and Joshs storage shed. Simon finds a croquet set in need of restoration.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In this episode, Simon accidently puts his foot through a drum, while Henry decides hes a silver surfer. They also uncover a gramophone and a stow-away bed to restyle.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Gary and Alison are looking for a rural holiday home in the Haute-Vienne area of central France. Can Laura Hamilton find them the perfect place for their budget of £100k?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sharon and Brendon want to buy a holiday home beside beautiful Lake Garda, but they each have very different priorities... Can Lucy Alexander fulfil their dreams?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jill and Ronnie are finally taking the plunge and moving to France. Jasmine Harman lines up five superb options in Languedoc-Roussillon for their £250k budget, but are any perfect for them?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jaazzmina and Mark want to create a remote Spanish smallholding and wellness retreat in Almería Province. Can Sara Damergi find the right spot, for £100,000?

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Scooby the Great Dane seeks a new recruit for the gang. Its a double date for the Davies family with a Labrador and German Shepherd. And is Fox the Pomeranian about to get lucky?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Hatton Garden: The Great Diamond Heist
programme.desc: In 2015, a gang of ageing criminals pulled off a multi-million heist in Londons Hatton Garden diamond district. What happened next? And where are the stolen millions?

programme.title: Stormbreaker
programme.desc: Following the suspicious death of his uncle, 14-year-old Alex Rider is forcibly recruited into MI6. After a gruelling period of training the schoolboy turned reluctant super spy is sent on his first mission. Will it be his last?

programme.title: Taskmaster: Champion of Champions
programme.desc: Theyve already won Gregs golden noggin. But which series supremo can go one better? Andy Zaltzman, John Robins, Maisie Adam, Mathew Baynton and Sam Campbell do battle.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Security guard Weston manages a violent incident with a patient, revealing the challenges of working on the hospital frontline. 33-year-old Jack is rushed into AandE after falling from a horse. And seven-year-old Lyla has a finger infection.

programme.title: Chisum
programme.desc: A rugged cattle baron takes on an unscrupulous entrepreneur who is determined to subdue the people of Pecos, New Mexico, and take over the cattlemans land. programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Phil Broughton pulls over a young lad in an old Vauxhall thats suspected of being on cloned plates. The biggest surprise of all comes when Phil discovers its the drivers 15th birthday.

programme.title: Mud Mountain Haulers

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in the West
programme.desc: A railway magnate hires a ruthless killer to murder a family whose farm is on the rail route, but reckons without the subsequent arrival of the farmers young widow and a mysterious stranger intent on tracking down the killer.

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britains Speeders
programme.desc: On the M180 near Scunthorpe, a driver seems more concerned about losing his footwear than getting nicked. A PC hunts for a suspected cloned car linked to a recent burglary late at night.

programme.title: Motorway: Hell on the Highway
programme.desc: The consequences of driving in extreme weather, from a tornado wreaking havoc on a camper van in Arkansas, to a battle royale in Virginia between a flash flood and a hybrid car.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: Eugene Tooms, the genetic mutant, is released from the sanatorium and Mulder becomes personally involved to ensure that he is caught in the act of getting the last human liver he needs to go into hibernation.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Americas Speeders
programme.desc: Patrol Officer Nick Garruto is in hot pursuit of car thieves; a suspected drugs trafficker is stopped by Patrolman Bill Gibson; a wobbly driver has one too many margaritas.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Carl Andrews is at the wheel of a gargantuan load, Russells mobile classroom gets shaken and his engine cooked, and the Ferris dodge raging bush fires.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Marcus Charman and colleague Trigger patch up an HGVs fuel tank during evening rush hour, Tez resorts to using a blowtorch to fix a car transporters snapped spring, and a broken down driver gives out the wrong location.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: On one of the UKs busiest roads, a jackknifed HGV has careered through a front garden, over a ditch and blocked a lane of traffic. Meanwhile, Jims mettle is tested and Steve has to quell a rebellion.

programme.title: Fights, Camera, Action!
programme.desc: Whether raging at a cheating partner, a rude stranger or a figure of authority, women everywhere are physically venting their anger. And more people than ever are capturing these incidents on their phones and posting them online.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Underdog Gets Away
programme.desc: Monicas first Thanksgiving dinner for the gang goes horribly wrong when they all get locked on the roof after watching a giant runaway balloon from the New York Thanksgiving parade. Elsewhere, Joey wins his first modelling gig as the poster boy for a venereal disease campaign, and Ross competes with his ex-wife to communicate with their unborn baby.

programme.sub-title: The One With the Candy Hearts

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Stoned Guy
programme.desc: The owner of a fancy restaurant where Monica applies for the chefs position is a little high on illicit substances when he tries the gourmet feast she prepares for him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe doesnt get on with her identical twin Ursula. Unfortunately, no one can tell them apart, which leads to a few cases of mistaken identity, not least for Joey who is quite taken with Ursula.

programme.title: Cats And Dogs At War
programme.desc: Cats and Dogs at War meets families whose fighting pets have turned their homes into a daily battlefield. With no end to hostilities insight, distraught owners call on animal behaviour experts Chirag Patel and Nanci Creedon to help secure a truce.

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Master dog trainer Graeme Hall is on a quest to restore harmony, and with more than 10 years experience, has built his reputation on finding quick but long-lasting fixes to almost any dog behaviour problem.

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Can Graeme Hall stop a Labradoodles dirty obsession, and also curtail Ziggy from ransacking his owners house? Plus, the basics of sit and stay - but without having to say a word.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: A violent new crime wave known as County Lines is taking hold in Britain. Across the country, urban drug gangs are expanding their empires by moving into small towns and rural areas.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Targeting crossborder criminals is a priority for the traffic cops as a fifth of the offences which North Yorkshire Police deal with, like theft and drug-dealing, are committed by crooks from outside the area.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: In York, early-morning raids are carried out on two properties seeking suspects who are believed to be exploiting teenage girls. At Wetherby services on the A1M, seven stowaways are found in a lorry that has recently arrived from the continent.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: A tyre stinger is used to stop a vehicle suspected of carrying stolen mobile phones. Automatic Number Plate Recognition is used to track and intercept a vehicle suspected of being used by burglars targeting the homes of older folk.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: In this episode, Jim intercepts a car containing cannabis, a late-night reveller talks himself into trouble and Sergeant Baxter has his work cut out when confronted by a dishonest driver.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The interceptors stop an uninsured driver with a violent past, Jon Peacock and police dog Brad hunt for suspected burglars, Sarah Capes gets a whiff of something illegal, PC Templeman pulls over a faulty white van and Dan Brigginshaw uncovers a drivers stash.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The hunt for a suspected burglar takes an unexpected turn, a late-night stop reveals a bag of drugs and Jon has a tussle with a fallen tree. Barry deals with a case of some very hot dogs locked inside a car.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Dan is in hot pursuit of a two-wheeled tearaway, a suspected drunk driver nearly causes a calamity and Mark has to turn Colombo at a three-car collision. Meanwhile, Sarah has a communication meltdown with a Mercedes driver.

programme.title: Crime of Passion? The Trial of Jane Andrews
programme.desc: The shocking trial, in which the former dresser to the Duchess of York was accused of brutally murdering her boyfriend, that drove a wedge through a privileged set.

programme.title: Accused: Guilty or Innocent?
programme.sub-title: Jealous Killer or Fearful Victim? Part 1
programme.desc: A man suffers a violent beating by his ex-wifes new partner. The brawl ends with the attackers killing. Did the accused kill in self-defence or murder a love rival?

programme.title: Phrogging: Hider in My House
programme.desc: Tina Bowen believes her dead mother is playing tricks on the family until a battle with a flesh-and-blood intruder uncovers a real-life horror movie; Amber Dawn feels uneasy in her new apartment; her dog barks at nothing and lights turn on and off.

programme.title: My Lover My Killer
programme.desc: When Guida Rufino met Anthony Roberts she finally got a baby. But only a few years later the couple would both end up dead in a frenzied attack that left a child without its parents.

programme.title: Skin AandE
programme.desc: The teams new dermatologist Dr Magnus Lynch specialises in facial surgery and makes an explosive start with a large jawline cyst. Plus a man with infectious warts.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Nurse Chloe treats 88-year-old Enid, who has been knocked over by high-speed winds and has a severe open head wound. Meanwhile, doctor Mason and doctor Staunton meet 44-year-old Darren who has been rushed in suffering from severe chest pains.

programme.title: Attenboroughs Life in Colour
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the extraordinary ways that some animals use colour to hide and disappear into the background. New science reveals how the Bengal tiger in central India uses its orange-black stripes to hide from its colour-blind prey. In Kenyas Masai Mara, the zebras black-and-white pattern confuses predators with an extraordinary effect called motion dazzle. And on the island of Cuba, a small snail uses colourful stripes in a surprising way to hide from its enemies.

programme.title: Secret Garden

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.desc: Rural treasures stolen and livestock lost - police uncover crimes beneath the soil.

programme.title: SAS: Catching the Criminals
programme.desc: Ex-SAS leader Billy Billingham examines the applications of military techniques in crime-fighting and offers his insights to help law enforcement agencies achieve better results.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.sub-title: Celebrity Special with Martin and Carol Smillie
programme.desc: Carol Smillie is in for a shock when she sees the state of the property Martin shows her around in Scotland. We time-travel back to 2006 when Martin saw a house in a stunning location in Wiltshire. Jacqui is in Warwickshire where outside space is at a premium.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two families go head-to-head at a busy antiques fair in Staffordshire while their experts John Cameron and Irita Marriott try to turn their purchases into profits at auction.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: BBC London News
programme.desc: News, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Roman Kemp meets childrens entertainer Susan and suggests she add a hot bath to her evening routine. Not only could it improve her quality of sleep - it could even reduce the risk of heart disease.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team take on two gardens. Chris and Amelia are in Scunthorpe to convert married couple Susan and Joes large bland plot into a romantic country garden to offset Joes metal animal collection that currently dominates the space. Susan wants to bring some colourful planting into the space, with lots of scent, that will complement Joes metal sculptures. Lee and Flo are in Doncaster to transform John and Alasdairs bland new-build plot into a Eurovision-winning garden.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Theres a marvellous multi-generational move to consider as Alistair Appleton helps one family, spread over four generations, find two houses in one village. Its a tall order, but Alistair presents a whole host of interesting property options.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Peter Rabbit and his three sisters -- Flopsy, Mopsy and Cotton-Tail -- enjoy spending their days in Mr. McGregors vegetable garden. When one of McGregors relatives suddenly moves in, hes less than thrilled to discover a family of rabbits in his new home. A battle of wills soon breaks out as the new owner hatches scheme after scheme to get rid of Peter -- a resourceful rabbit who proves to be a worthy and wily opponent.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Tom Allen for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Celebrity Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Entrepreneur and winner of `The Apprentice, Tim Campbell, is pausing his search for the business worlds next big thing, to focus on finding a new life in the countryside. Joined by his wife, Jas, theyre in the safe hands of Sonali Shah.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jasmine receives a visitor, Nicola is left frustrated, and Ross and Harvey hatch a plan.

programme.title: Babies

programme.title: Ambulance

programme.title: Attenboroughs Life in Colour
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the extraordinary ways that some animals use colour to hide and disappear into the background. programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team take on two gardens. Chris and Amelia are in Scunthorpe to convert married couple Susan and Joes large bland plot into a romantic country garden to offset Joes metal animal collection that currently dominates the space. Susan wants to bring some colourful planting into the space, with lots of scent, that will complement Joes metal sculptures. Lee and Flo are in Doncaster to transform John and Alasdairs bland new-build plot into a Eurovision-winning garden.

programme.title: Escape to the Country

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Peter Rabbit and his three sisters -- Flopsy, Mopsy and Cotton-Tail -- enjoy spending their days in Mr. McGregors vegetable garden. When one of McGregors relatives suddenly moves in, hes less than thrilled to discover a family of rabbits in his new home. A battle of wills soon breaks out as the new owner hatches scheme after scheme to get rid of Peter -- a resourceful rabbit who proves to be a worthy and wily opponent.

programme.title: Attenboroughs Life in Colour
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the extraordinary ways that some animals use colour to hide and disappear into the background. New science reveals how the Bengal tiger in central India uses its orange-black stripes to hide from its colour-blind prey. In Kenyas Masai Mara, the zebras black-and-white pattern confuses predators with an extraordinary effect called motion dazzle. And on the island of Cuba, a small snail uses colourful stripes in a surprising way to hide from its enemies.

Chris and Amelia are in Scunthorpe to convert married couple Susan and Joes large bland plot into a romantic country garden to offset Joes metal animal collection that currently dominates the space. programme.title: Disclosure
programme.desc: Disclosure investigates financial allegations against a private care home operator. After an undercover investigation exposed failures at the same chain, families across the country reveal lengthy battles to recoup money from the people who were supposed to care for their loved ones. They tell us that they were met with a wall of silence, hamstrung by a gap in regulation and even threatened for speaking out. The company says it complies with regulations.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: After a three-minute video featuring a female prison officer and an inmate having sex inside HMP Wandsworth goes viral, award-winning journalist Mobeen Azhar begins an investigation into what happened and why. A year of twists, turns and damning revelations pulls back the curtain on a dangerous wider world of coercion, corruption and contraband thriving behind bars.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: As Mobeens investigation deepens, it becomes clearer that illicit relationships between prisoners and jail staff are just the tip of the iceberg. The bigger story involves organised criminal gangs determined to cash in on a lucrative prison drug economy. When news breaks of a prison governor being jailed for a relationship with a drug-dealing gang boss, Mobeen becomes determined to uncover the true extent of criminal influence inside our prisons.

programme.title: P Diddy: The Rise and Fall
programme.desc: Yinka Bokinni investigates how Sean Combs has gone from being the worlds biggest hip-hop mogul to haemorrhaging money, businesses, friends and fans in a matter of months. The worlds biggest hip-hop moguls legacy is now indelibly tarnished.

programme.desc: Stepping into the beautiful gardens in Oxfordshire today are Jaycee from West Sussex and Tristan from Perthshire, both hoping to turn their love for their crafts into successful businesses. Ex-HR worker Jaycee made the brave decision to give up the day job to concentrate full time on her upcycling business and hopes that Dom and his team of mentors can help. Mentor Zoe is worried that Jaycee is focusing on the wrong items and is keen for her to rethink her way forward.

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Gardening

programme.title: Great British Menu

programme.title: Easter Parade
programme.desc: Don Hewes, one half of a great dancing team, is dumped by his partner the day before the Easter parade. Wounded, Don finds himself a new partner, chorus girl Hannah Brown, who he decides to make into a star in time for the following parade. Don switches his affection easily from Nadine to Hannah, but keeps his distance so he wont be hurt again. Hannah loves Don too, but thinks hes still in love with Nadine.

programme.title: High Society
programme.desc: A remake of `The Philadelphia Story, this film is about the society wedding of Tracy Lord to priggish George Kittredge. Her ex-husband lives next door, and is organising a jazz festival for the same weekend. The attentions of a reporter and his photographer cause her to rethink her upcoming nuptuals.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: A young tigress is chosen to found a new dynasty. Airlifted from her home in an Indian park to life in a new reserve, Baghani the tigress must fight with leopards for territory and learn to hunt dangerous wild boar. Also released is Rajore, a hot blooded young male. For two years every aspect of their lives are followed by the camera. Will they mate and start a family, or will they be killed by poachers?

programme.title: Burma with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon journeys up the vast Irrawaddy river to the old royal capital of Mandalay, home to an exotic market for precious jade. In Burmas mountainous highlands, he experiences Burmas vibrant ethnic heritage at an extraordinary and explosive fire balloon festival, then visits an elephant sanctuary and finds out how a growing tourism industry is helping them survive after working for decades in the logging industry.

programme.desc: Genius British logician and cryptologist Alan Turing helps crack Germanys Enigma Code during World War II, but is later prosecuted by his government for illegal homosexual acts.

programme.title: Being There
programme.desc: Black comedy about American gullibility in which a withdrawn old man, appropriately called Chance, is seized upon as some kind of oracle. His elevation to the status of a national celebrity is triggered by a road accident, in which Chance finds himself under a car driven by the wife of a political kingmaker. Soon his charm and honesty have won him the ears of people in the highest public offices.

So they go back outside, where the billion, zillion stars and a huge moon are waiting for them.

programme.title: Hey Duggee

Wait a minute - the egg is running off! Can the Squirrels help bring it back to the chicken safely? Theres a big world out there to explore so the Squirrels need to be quick. Oh, and they must make sure they find the right egg to return!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are at the Clubhouse playing, but they cant stop thinking about the dreams they had last night. programme.title: Friends

programme.title: Friends

programme.title: Murdertown

programme.title: Nightmare in Suburbia

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers

programme.title: Homestead Rescue

programme.title: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: Marty re-rescues the Broadhead family, whose Ohio home was made from 30,000 tires and faces major new structural issues. Taking on a big challenge with limited time, Marty must restore a settling berm and construct a concrete storm.

programme.desc: Tim and Howard race to complete a thousand-gallon order before the Canadian tariff-running boat docks. Josh takes cover as surveyors set stakes above his underground still site. Mike and Jerry tail their bootlegger and uncover a backroads speakeasy.

programme.desc: Work continues on the Volkswagen delivery bus with new motor, suspension, stance, wheels and tyres.

programme.title: Shortys Dream Shop

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.sub-title: Uranus and Neptune: Rise of the Ice Giants

programme.title: How the Universe Works

programme.desc: Archaeologists excavating a historic Chilean site make a chilling discovery and find decapitated human skulls separate from a nearby burial site; experts use advanced cranial forensics to reveal what happened to these ancient, butchered victims.

programme.title: Ancient Unexplained Files
programme.desc: An archaeological excavation uncovers ancient human carnage; experts use 3D modelling to reveal what may be the first major war in human history.

programme.title: Expedition X

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Maitre d Fred and his team open the doors of the First Dates restaurant to more hopeful singletons, including 38-year-old party boy Danny, straight-talking hairdresser Lauren, 33-year-old liver bird Emma and 39-year-old ex-Navy gunner Ian.

programme.title: First Dates

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Thai Stag Do
programme.desc: In a bid to save money to put on a show-stopping wedding for Jay, his down-to-earth bride, Jack-the-lad Ian flies himself and his two best men 12,000 miles away to Thailand.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Rock-star turned zoo-keeper Scott spends under £100 on Ebay for a wedding dress for his paramedic bride to be, Verity - leaving him £1, 800 to spend on life-size plastic animals to decorate the reception for his jungle themed wedding.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Sea-loving groom, Dave wants to whisk his water-phobic bride-to-be, Rosie off to a remote island wedding, but its not just any island, its windswept Lundy Island off the coast of Devon, where the bride once nearly drowned and had to be rescued.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Situated near Lake Tahoe, Squaw Valley is one of the largest ski resorts in the United States. CEO Andy Wirth has been busy merging Squaw Valley with newly acquired mountain resort Alpine Meadows. He dons a disguise to go undercover and find out how the combined operation is running.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Based in Los Angeles, Fatburger is known as the `last great hamburger stand. It began in the 1950s as a late-night hangout for musicians and quickly grew to international success. Andy Wiederhorn, CEO of Fatburger, disguises himself to go incognito among his employees.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Belfast, Gemma and her `wee-bald man husband Jim serve up a very healthy menu that caters for pescatarians as well carnivores. Second to host are Leeann and her husband Micheal, and finally, Matt and Paul lay on some unique dance routines.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Newlywed am-dram lovers Mel and Richard raise the curtain for this competition in Belfast. Joining them are super-competitive Cherith and David, and rockers done good, Joanne and Jim.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Oasis tribute singer Degsy and his wife Jackie kick things off with a Manchester-themed menu. Second night hosts Jo and Vikki serve an Indian-themed menu, and the final night sees Chris and Bex stepping up to the plate.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: South African-born Nigel, and his wife Nikki, kick off the competition in South Wales. Day two sees Darren and Simon attempt to woo their guests with a flavour of their first-class lifestyle, and finally its Neil and Carlys chance to impress.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Ramsay returns to some of the most memorable restaurants in the past to see if theyve implemented positive changes.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to America in a bid to rescue failing restaurant owners from imminent disaster and provide them with a recipe for success. In this first episode, Gordon visits the Hot Potato Cafe in Fishtown, Philadelphia. With no profit made in its two years of trading and an influential restaurant reviewer dubbing them `Spuddy Hell, Gordon soon realises Hot Potato may have had its chips.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon visits Flamangos in rural Whitehouse, New Jersey. Mother and daughter owners Adele and Cheryl are stuck in their tropical ways but their restaurant is far from paradise. They are in deep financial trouble and Adele struggles to get on with her staff, is disappointed by her chef and hates Gordon with a passion. Gordon has one last chance to bring them all around and make the restaurant a success.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon visits Bazzini restaurant in Ridgewood, New Jersey. Owner Paul Bazzini was an award-winning chef in New York who decided to run his own restaurant but he never realised how difficult the change would be. Now, he finds everyday tasks overwhelming and his passion for food is in danger of being extinguished by the daily grind. However, passion isnt the only problem in this failing restaurant.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon visits Mojito, a Cuban cafe in Brooklyn, New York. Owners Marcelo and Kata used to be married to each other but now fight over everything. Seven years ago, the couple were happy newlyweds and the restaurant was their shared dream, but now the passion has gone and theyre constantly at each others throats, with their staff getting caught in the crossfire.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Rosie Jones take on Joe Wilkinson and Phil Wang. Dr John Cooper Clarke joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts a festive edition of the comedy panel show. Rob Beckett, Tom Allen and Liam Charles take on Katherine Ryan, Scarlett Moffatt and Maisie Adam.

programme.title: Jordan Gray: Is It a Bird?
programme.desc: Jordan brings her comedy to TV for the first time. Shot in her home town theatre in Essex, this transgender stand up special is a whirlwind of music and comedy covering politics to Batman.

programme.title: Black Books
programme.desc: Manny is extremely proud of the travel writing festival that he has organised - everybody loves Jason the explorer. Meanwhile, Bernard hires an assassin to take care of his neighbour.

programme.title: Black Books
programme.desc: Manny and Fran are determined to go out and do something for once, and even persuade Bernard to go to a party. However, a nasty surprise awaits Bernard when he return home.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: A topical panel show based on a huge series of opinion polls carried out around Britain. Jason Biggs, Nicola Adams, Nick Helm and Rob Beckett guest.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: A topical panel show based on a huge series of opinion polls carried out around Britain. With guests Micky Flanagan, Stephen Mangan, Natalie Cassidy and Humphrey Ker.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Armando Montelongo, the chief executive officer of Armando Montelongo Companies, hires a body double to help conceal his identity while under cover.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Gigi Butler, the chief executive officer of Gigis Cupcakes, struggles with the pressures of cake decorating while under cover.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: Beth and Chris, determined to give their four-year-old twins the best possible education, look to move to a better district.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In West Yorkshire, Sophie and Harj plan a Mexican menu, if Harj can find his way around the kitchen. Richard and Libby stick to British classics. Butcher Sean and Jane serve a meat feast.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Gill and Mark, Matt and Derek, and Cat and Martin compete to host the best dinner party in Glasgow, in a week featuring an impromptu sing-song, a kitchen disaster and an MC battle.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One in a Hot Air Balloon
programme.desc: Another brave bride leaves the most important day of her life in the hands of the man she loves. Romantic groom David met the woman of his dreams, Elle, just over a year ago. Now he wants to quite literally sweep Elle off her feet, with an ambitious wedding in a hot air balloon and his bride wearing a dress he has designed himself. But with balloon flights being notoriously weather-dependent and a feisty fiancee and her eight bridesmaids to please, will Davids air-bound nuptials take off?

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One in a Car Park
programme.desc: James, the groom who confesses he doesnt actually want a wedding, plans the biggest day of bride-to-be Hollies life. Whilst she dreams of a local church wedding back home in rural Lincolnshire, James wants a rooftop party in a multistory car park.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: The boss of Marcos Pizza goes undercover and meets a delivery driver who works so many hours he virtually lives in his truck and misses watching his child grow up.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Greg Adler, the boss of 4 Wheel Parts, goes undercover within his own business and discovers that his training standards are not being met.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay tries to improve the family-run Fiesta Sunrise, a Mexican restaurant in West Nyack, N.Y., with a cockroach-infested kitchen.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Ramsey tackles spoiled food, fake fish and a small customer base when he visits Sante La Brea in the heart of Hollywood.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: This time, the brothers step in to give Frank, Diane and their family a new start by transforming their tired old property into a luxury home.

programme.title: Below Deck

programme.title: Oliver!
programme.desc: Endearing film version of Lionel Barts successful stage musical based on Dickenss tale of the fluctuating fortunes of an orphan boy. Ron Moody stands out as the wily old scrounger Fagin in a series of well-crafted set-pieces and atmospheric musical numbers, and a tribe of child stars brings the 19th-century London underworld vividly to life.

programme.title: Bohemian Rhapsody Interview Special
programme.desc: Rami Malek discusses playing Freddie Mercury in the rock biopic about the rise of the band Queen. Preceded by the Queen-scored sci-fi adventure Flash Gordon.

programme.title: The Poseidon Adventure
programme.desc: A priest leads a rag-tag group of survivors on a quest to escape the capsized ocean liner Poseidon. On their way to the safety of the propeller shaft, which has become their only route to survival, they must overcome fire, steam and lots of water.

programme.title: Bohemian Rhapsody Interview Special
programme.desc: Rami Malek discusses playing Freddie Mercury in the rock biopic about the rise of the band Queen. Preceded by the Queen-scored sci-fi adventure Flash Gordon.

programme.title: Sister Act 2: Back in the Habit
programme.desc: Sequel in which nightclub singer Deloris Van Cartier is asked to get back into her habit when her old friends at St Catherines Convent find difficulty in controlling a school filled with juvenile delinquents. Convincing everyone that Deloris is a nun is going to be a challenge in itself, especially with a group of incompetent monks trying to hinder their efforts.

programme.title: Bohemian Rhapsody Interview Special
programme.desc: Rami Malek discusses playing Freddie Mercury in the rock biopic about the rise of the band Queen. Preceded by the Queen-scored sci-fi adventure Flash Gordon.

programme.title: Central Intelligence
programme.desc: A lethal CIA agent and his former classmate encounter shootouts, espionage and double-crosses while trying to save the U.S. spy satellite system.

programme.title: Cocaine Bear
programme.desc: An oddball group of cops, criminals, tourists and teens converges in a Georgia forest after a 500-pound black bear ingests a large amount of cocaine and goes on a drug-fueled rampage.

programme.title: The G
programme.desc: Ann Hunter and her husband are snatched from their home by a corrupt legal guardian. They are put in a prison-like eldercare facility. Trapped in a corrupt system, "THE G" begins to fight to get them out and get revenge on the people who did this.

programme.title: Public Enemies
programme.desc: Its 1933 and as the Great Depression grips America, the exploits of Tommy Gun-toting outlaws have gripped the nations imagination. Dubbed `Public Enemies by crusading FBI director J Edgar Hoover, the bank-robbing rogues have become Robin Hood figures to the impoverished members of the population. Public enemy number one is none other than John Dillinger, an inveterate heist expert.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Italy
programme.desc: Adam is in Bari to taste the most iconic foods named after this city. He tastes the simplest and delicious focaccia Barese, and as seafood is king here, Adam savours tiella Barese - a hearty dish of land and sea made with potatoes, rice and mussels.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Italy
programme.desc: Adam is in Rome to taste the most iconic foods named after this city. He tucks into one of the best local pasta dishes, cacio e pepe, and porchetta Romana, which is suckling pig Rome style. Plus, he tries gelato, the way the Romans make it.

programme.title: Mary Berrys Easter Feast
programme.desc: Mary discovers the traditions followed by diverse British families and communities during Lent and Good Friday and the food that brings them together as each prepare for the great Easter celebration.

programme.title: Mary Berrys Easter Feast
programme.desc: Mary explores the wonderful foods that bring each different community together on Easter Sunday, the most symbolic and meaningful feast in the Christian calendar. As well as sharing her own family favourites, like mouth-watering Roast Lamb, Mary discovers how the Greek Orthodox community break the fast with Tsoureki bread; spends a day with the Archbishop of York, John Sentamu, cooking up a storm in his kitchen; and discovers Filipino and Italian Easter specialities.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Phoenix, a regional pork dish, in Toronto, Canada, sticky Asian pork ribs and a Canadian staple.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Bake Off is back as 12 new bakers enter the iconic white tent. Its cake week, and the bakers tackle a striking sponge and a scaled-down version of a home close to their hearts.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Biscuit Week, and the bakers kick off with illusion macarons in the Signature, before producing a fruity favourite in the Technical. And in the Showstopper, they put their creativity to the test by making a 3D mask entirely from biscuits.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Bread Week, and the bakers put their stamp on a classic pizza, get in a twist with a classic pastry and tackle a showstopping Smorgåstårta, a decorative Danish sandwich cake.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Mexican Week, and the bakers take on the sweet bread, Pan Dulce, and a Mexican street food staple, and make a showstopping version of the milk-soaked Tres Leches cake.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Dessert Week and the bakers get all steamed up in the Signature, tackle a classic lemon meringue pie, and create a showstopping sponge cake with a hidden surprise.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Halloween Week and the bakers make apple cake, a smore-ish Technical and a spooky showstopping piñata. Whose bakes will be eerie-sistible enough to earn them star baker?

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Custard Week and the bakers put their twist on floating islands in the Signature, tackle a summer staple, and make custard the star and basis of a showstopping set gateau.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Pastry Week, and the bakers are tasked with making vol-au-vents in the Signature and a delicious savoury snack in the Technical, before creating a showstopping 3D pie-scape depicting their favourite childhood story.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its the semi-final and the perfect time to test the remaining bakers abilities in patisserie. There are mini charlottes in the Signature that demand precision, a vertical challenge in the Technical, and finally an opulent Swedish showstopper.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its the final, and the last remaining bakers are tasked with making a perfect picnic for the Signature, and for the Technical, a summer classic. And in the final Showstopper, they must create a baked celebration of our planet.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey sails to Philadelphia to tackle the epic Classic Whale Challenge, a 6 kg bagel sandwich stuffed with cream cheese, nova salmon, whitefish, tomatoes and onions.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey travels to Seattle where he climbs a Mount Fuji-sized burger and takes on seven terrifyingly spicy Buffalo wings that have defeated over 5,000 challengers.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guys sampling foods straight off the grill. In Los Angeles, theres a burger joint spicing things up with a hot pork sandwich, and going Mediterranean with a lamb burger.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guys checking out some unique combos. In Korea Town, Los Angeles, Guy visits a craft beer joint putting duck into their fries as well as in the French dip.

programme.desc: Join Jonathan Bernis and a host of guests, journeying through Scripture from a Jewish perspective and meeting God along the way.

programme.title: God TV

programme.title: Rev It Up with Dr. Sam Kojoglanian
programme.desc: As a cardiologist, evangelist, medical missionary, and author, Samuel Kojoglanian, MD (Dr. Sam) serves the Lord and mankind in the name of Jesus and the power of the Holy Spirit.

programme.title: Studio 5
programme.desc: An inside look at movies, music, sports and television through in-depth interviews with artists at the forefront of the entertainment industry.

programme.title: Glorious Moments -- Prophet Jerome
programme.desc: Bishop HE Rt Rev Prophet Dr Jerome Fernando answered the call of God over his life at a young age, and since then has had a heart to draw people to their rightful destinies in the Lord.

programme.title: Oxford Bible Church
programme.desc: Led by Derek and Hilary Walker, OBC aims to bring love, hope and healing to the world by teaching Gods Word in the power of the Holy Spirit. Derek is an Oxford graduate, author and End-Time expert.

programme.title: Love Israel with Baruch Korman PH.D
programme.desc: Verse-by-verse Bible study, going through the Old and New Testaments with Baruch Korman.

programme.title: Christian World News
programme.desc: An international world news programme bringing you the latest in Christian world news.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its decision time for the chefs from Northern Ireland. One chef has left the competition and only two remain. Who will cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton? They will be joined by celebrity guest judge writer and performer Charlie Higson and the judges arent easily pleased.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Welsh chefs raise the curtain with their starters, but which one is going to produce a showstopper? All three contestants have created sophisticated plates of food with simple dish titles. Will veteran Jeremy Lee be impressed with Richard Daviess chicken salad or will Mary Ann Gilchrists peas and ham or Luke Thomass ham, egg and pineapple get them off to a flying start?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Welsh chefs cook their fish course. Richard Davies serves up grilled and tartare of mackerel, while Mary Ann Gilchrist serves a lobster dish and calls it `The Great Escape. Luke Thomas is hoping that his quirky take on a classic fish finger will impress veteran Jeremy Lee too.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the main course and rabbit, rose veal and venison are all dished up for `Great British Menu veteran Richard Corrigan. Plus, one of the chefs is joined by `Eastenders star June Brown for a fundraising challenge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course. Will a strawberry and coconut dessert, a frozen white chocolate dish or apple and cherries come out on top? `Great British Menu veteran judge Richard Corrigan will decide who goes through to the judges tomorrow.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is decision time for the chefs from London and the South East. One chef left the competition last time and only two remain. They will cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. This time they will be joined by celebrity guest judge, comedienne and writer Arabella Weir.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is finals week and 24 of the nations finest chefs have been whittled down to just eight. Over the course of four hour-long shows, they will battle it out in the kitchen for the chance to get a dish to the Comic Relief Banquet. Actress Patricia Hodge, one of the stars of sitcom `Miranda, joins judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort to taste all eight winning regional starter courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their fish courses. They are all vying to finish in the top three and still be in with a chance of cooking at the special banquet. Tensions are high but there are still plenty of laughs as new dishes are introduced under competition rules, including a Sicilian influenced dish called the `Cod Father. Impressionist Ronni Ancona joins the judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Comedian Ade Edmondson tucks into the chefs main courses to help decide which three will remain in contention to cook at the special banquet. However, the chefs are in for a major surprise as the judges deliver unexpected news about the days competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its judgement day as the chefs not only learn the top three desserts but the judges announce the winning menu. `Fast Show writer and performer Charlie Higson helps judge the puddings. Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort decide on the four winning dishes, but there is a problem - they are torn between two dishes for one of the courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the climax of the competition as the winning chefs cook at the Comic Relief Banquet at the Royal Albert Hall. Special guest and co-founder of the charity Richard Curtis welcomes guests - the unsung heroes of charity work, as well as those who have benefited from their help and some of the celebrities who have inspired us to do something funny for money.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three top chefs from Scotland are out to impress this weeks veteran judge and be crowned Scottish champion. Adam Handling has already won a number of industry awards and is full of confidence. His starter sets out to take the diner on a tour of the UKs best producers. Hes up against Michael Bremner, who is hoping to impress with contemporary ingredients including Birch sap and Douglas fir oil. Ally McGrath is celebrating his nations finest produce and making his very own version of haggis.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three newcomers to the competition create their fish dishes in a bid to be crowned Scottish champion. The standard is so high the veteran judge is moved to tears.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Ally McGrath has been keeping the veteran judge entertained with his very individual approach. Also, the highly competitive Adam Handling has been showcasing the finest produce grown and caught in and around the UK in his menu. Once again Michael Bremner is out to show how inventive he can be.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: One chef will go home and today the veteran judge finds himself with a difficult decision to make. Inventive Chef Michael Bremner is recreating the Scottish deserts of his childhood, Adam Handling is sticking with his trademark Asian flavours and Ally McGrath is tempering chocolate for the very first time.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scotlands two highest scoring chefs are cooking for judges for the very first time. Both chefs have decided to make changes to some of their dishes, taking on board criticisms made during the week by the veteran judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chef Josh Eggleton is back for a third time. Last year he made the national finals when his starter was shortlisted for the banquet, this year he intends to go one step further and cook at the banquet. One of his rivals last time, Jude Kereama is also back and out to do better than he did previously. The third competitor is newcomer Chris Wheeler who is originally from Swanage in Dorset.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: There are some exciting dishes on the menu as the three chefs from the South West tackle the fish course. Chef Josh Eggleton is hoping to impress with an original take on Bolognese, just one of six elements on his complex fish dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the main course and Chef Chris Wheeler is out to show just how theatrical he can be, his ideas are surprising everyone in the kitchen. No one knows quite what to expect and the veteran chef is hoping it tastes as good as it looks.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the dessert course and it is Josh Eggleton who has come up with the most ambitious dessert. He is creating his own self-serve ice cream made from vegetables, he is making everything from scratch including toppings and syrups.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the head to head and the South West regions two highest scoring chefs are cooking for judges Oliver Peyton, Prue Leith and Matthew Fort. They are joined by guest judge, journalist and restaurant critic, Grace Dent.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Two newcomers to the competition are challenging reigning North West champion Matt Worswick. Adam Reid is Head Chef for one of the UKs leading culinary talents, and Kim Woodward who made history when she was appointed Head Chef at the Savoy Grill.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and reigning North West champion Matt Worswick has reason to be confident. However, Kims confident she has got one up on Matt by baking her own black bread unlike Matt whos taking a shortcut.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the main course of the North West Regional heat and the scores mean this is a crucial round. Matt Worswick may be the reigning champion but he is not yet assured of a place cooking for the judges and he is feeling the strain.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course and the three North West competitors are getting creative, producing some of the most original and stunning desserts. All three chefs are working in a hot kitchen, the tension is palpable, and this time one chef will go home.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs are cooking for judges Matthew Fort, Prue Leith and Oliver Peyton, and guest judge Lady Claire MacDonald OBE. There are stand out dishes from both chefs making it a difficult contest to judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the finest chefs from Wales are competing to be crowned champion. Two chefs are back for a second time: Phil Carmichael, from Cowbridge, South Wales, is Executive Chef at Berners Tavern in London and Adam Bannister, is chef and co-owner of Slice in his home city of Swansea. Adam was crowed Welsh champion last year so this year Phil is back with something to prove. He plans to go home raising the flag for Wales. The third chef is newcomer Andrew Birch, also from Swansea.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and newcomer Andrew Birch is confident, he is passionate about seafood. He is celebrating the cockle which is harvested near his home city of Swansea. Adam Bannister has created an alter ego, Captain Bannister for his modern take on the old boil in the bag fish dinners and Phil has chosen to revamp the classic prawn cocktail.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Phil Carmichael is serving up his version of an OBE, a refined take on onions, bacon and egg. Adam is planning to serve up Wild boar on a spit, while Andrew turns up the heat when he combines strong Japanese flavours with a traditional roast.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the dessert course and one chef will go home. Phil Carmichael is determined it wont be him but not everything is going to plan when he tackles his Cup of Tea for Maam. One of the accompaniments to his earl grey panna cotta just wont work. He has to make a difficult decision.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the head to head. The two highest scoring chefs from Wales are cooking for judges Oliver Peyton, Prue Leith and Matthew Fort who are joined by guest judge, blogger and food writer Rosie Birkett.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three newcomers are competing to become regional champion. Daniel Smith, a former head chef for the Roux brothers, now runs his own restaurant in his beloved home county of Norfolk. Andrew Scotts worked with the best in the business, and hes concentrating on telling powerful stories as well as delivering perfect flavours. Danny Gill is a former head chef at Daniel Cliffords two star restaurant Midsummer House.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and the chefs are using some of the UKs best seafood: lobster, salmon and Cornish crab. Danny Gill is a chef who likes to use the very latest equipment to create the most intense flavours. Hes going to great lengths to maximise the taste of his cucumber consomme and it is causing a lot of interest in the kitchen.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Two chefs are cooking venison. Danny Gill is making a venison wellington, inspired by his Dad, a former RAF chef who has served up Wellington for royalty at many air force banquets. While Andrew Scott has sourced his venison from the Queens Scottish estate, Balmoral.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: This week three chefs from the central region are fighting to be crowned champion. One competitor will go home, only the two highest scoring chefs will make it through to the judging chamber. Danny Gill is facing his toughest day yet, choosing to make a staggering eight elements for his imaginative dessert.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Central regions two highest scoring chefs are cooking for judges Oliver Peyton, Prue Leith and Matthew Fort, for the very first time. Joining them in the chamber is guest judge Enam Ali MBE, a food entrepreneur who set up The British Curry Awards.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the most exciting talents from London and the South East region are competing for a place in the National Finals. Head chef Mark Froydenlund runs Marcus Wareings two star restaurant. Russell Bateman is head Chef at The Grove in Hertfordshire and Ronnie Murray who heads up the Hix Restaurant group.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and chef Russell Bateman is celebrating the diversity of his beloved home town of London - by combining lobster with jerk spices for an ambitious fish course with seven key elements. Ronnie Murrays taking a risk with an ingredient which is close to the veteran judges heart...and is planning to serve it at three different temperatures warm, deep fried and frozen. And two Michelin starred Mark Froydenlund is planning a novel way to present his dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the all-important main course and returning contender Mark Froydenlund is banking on it being his strongest dish. Russell Bateman is creating a beef dish, and Ronnie Murrays mixing a crown of lamb with a curried filling.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the all-important main course and returning contender Mark Froydenlund is banking on it being his strongest dish. Russell Bateman is creating a beef dish, and Ronnie Murrays mixing a crown of lamb with a curried filling.

programme.title: NYC: Tornado Terror
programme.desc: Several swarming tornadoes cut a path of destruction so devastating and unpredictable it sends New York City into a frenzy. Now, in a race against Mother Nature, Deputy Mayor James Lawrence and his meteorologist wife, Cassie, must overcome their own marital disaster, and discover a way to calm the tornadoes fury.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts travels back to the Ages of Bronze and Iron to discover what kind of a place Britain was before the Romans invaded. With no written history, only archaeology can provide the clues. Alice uncovers a world that is complex, sophisticated and pretty strange. She examines the two Hebridean Bronze Age skeletons known as the Cladh Hallan Mummies.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts follows an entire year of British archaeology, joining up the results of digs and investigations the length of the country. The results are astonishing, and sometimes disturbing. Roman finds include the mystery of 97 babies murdered by the Thames, a fabulous Roman coin hoard found in Somerset and a man buried on a layer of dead animals.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In Norfolk, flint tools unearthed recently push the earliest human occupation back by 200,000 years, to around one million years ago. In Orkney an early farm yields glimpses of our ancestors earliest religious beliefs and customs.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Skeletons tell of both violent death and tenderness at the royal seat of power in Bamburgh, Northumbria, while at a remarkable community project at a shopping centre in Sittingbourne, people curate the grave goods of their own ancestors.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In the final episode Dr Alice Roberts goes in search of the Tudor age, a time that saw momentous changes across all aspects of British life. Along the way Alice visits excavations at Shakespeares first theatre in Londons Shoreditch, where the Bard began his career and `Romeo and Juliet was first performed. Alice also joins a team sifting through Shakespeares rubbish at his last home in Stratford-Upon-Avon.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Focusing on Roman Britannia, where finds include the thickening mystery of 97 baby skeletons found by the Thames, a newly discovered town in rural Devon that turns history on its head, and a Roman cult figure buried for 1700 years beneath a fort.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts travels back to the Viking Age in Britain and visits excavations that are revealing a different side to these seafaring pirates from Scandinavia. She looks for signs of the earliest Viking settlers in the Outer Hebrides, and in Orkney - where Viking dominance outlasted anywhere else in Britain - she visits the excavation of a Viking chiefs citadel and finds evidence of their way-of-life.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts travels back to the Ages of Bronze and Iron to discover what kind of a place Britain was before the Romans invaded. With no written history, only archaeology can provide the clues. Alice uncovers a world that is complex, sophisticated and pretty strange. She examines the two Hebridean Bronze Age skeletons known as the Cladh Hallan Mummies.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts goes in search of our elusive Stone Age ancestors. Along the way she visits the Channel island of Jersey where she meets a team of archaeologists hoping to shed new light on the much-maligned Neanderthals, and embarks on a kayak survey of the coastline looking for undiscovered sites hidden in the cliffs. At the Natural history museum Alice sees the dark side of our Ice Age ancestors lives.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts follows an entire year of British archaeology, joining up the results of digs and investigations the length of the country. The results are astonishing, and sometimes disturbing. Roman finds include the mystery of 97 babies murdered by the Thames, a fabulous Roman coin hoard found in Somerset and a man buried on a layer of dead animals.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In Norfolk, flint tools unearthed recently push the earliest human occupation back by 200,000 years, to around one million years ago. In Orkney an early farm yields glimpses of our ancestors earliest religious beliefs and customs.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Skeletons tell of both violent death and tenderness at the royal seat of power in Bamburgh, Northumbria, while at a remarkable community project at a shopping centre in Sittingbourne, people curate the grave goods of their own ancestors.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In the final episode Dr Alice Roberts goes in search of the Tudor age, a time that saw momentous changes across all aspects of British life. Along the way Alice visits excavations at Shakespeares first theatre in Londons Shoreditch, where the Bard began his career and `Romeo and Juliet was first performed. Alice also joins a team sifting through Shakespeares rubbish at his last home in Stratford-Upon-Avon.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Focusing on Roman Britannia, where finds include the thickening mystery of 97 baby skeletons found by the Thames, a newly discovered town in rural Devon that turns history on its head, and a Roman cult figure buried for 1700 years beneath a fort.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts travels back to the Viking Age in Britain and visits excavations that are revealing a different side to these seafaring pirates from Scandinavia. She looks for signs of the earliest Viking settlers in the Outer Hebrides, and in Orkney - where Viking dominance outlasted anywhere else in Britain - she visits the excavation of a Viking chiefs citadel and finds evidence of their way-of-life.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts travels back to the Ages of Bronze and Iron to discover what kind of a place Britain was before the Romans invaded. With no written history, only archaeology can provide the clues. Alice uncovers a world that is complex, sophisticated and pretty strange. She examines the two Hebridean Bronze Age skeletons known as the Cladh Hallan Mummies.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts goes in search of our elusive Stone Age ancestors. Along the way she visits the Channel island of Jersey where she meets a team of archaeologists hoping to shed new light on the much-maligned Neanderthals, and embarks on a kayak survey of the coastline looking for undiscovered sites hidden in the cliffs. At the Natural history museum Alice sees the dark side of our Ice Age ancestors lives.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a stone cottage in Tilton on the Hill in rural Leicestershire while Martin Roberts is at a property auction buy in Croydon on the outskirts of London and Lucy Alexander meets two friends who have bought a bungalow in Rainham in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mid-terrace house in Chaddesdon in Derby, a two-bedroom flat in Londons Maida Vale and a property in Warrington are all sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the new owners to hear what they have planned for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin looks round a pretty cottage with outbuildings with huge potential in Burton upon Trent, while Lucy checks out a flat in Londons Crystal Palace and Dion is in Mossley near Manchester in a four-bedroom bungalow.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin tackles the issue of solar panels on a property in Mountain Ash in Wales and wonders if itll be a sunny outcome for the new owner, while Lucy looks round a barn in Elstree in Hertfordshire and Dion visits a flat in Newport in Shropshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets a couple who buy a former millworkers cottage in Derbyshire as their first home together; Lucy Alexander meets a father and daughter team who are seasoned developers buying a house in Kent; and Dion Dublin talks to a man who, along with two friends, has clubbed together to buy an investment property in Lowestoft in Suffolk.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Walsall in the Midlands looking around a 1950s semi; Martin Roberts meets a man developing a ground-floor one-bedroom flat in Ilford in Essex; and Lucy Alexander catches up on a multi-million-pound redevelopment in East Horsley in Surrey where one small cottage with a big garden is converted into eight separate properties.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house with a bathroom layout challenge in Ripley, Derbyshire, a huge three-storey house with its freehold on offer in Erdington, Birmingham, and a mid-terrace house in Londons East Ham in need of a major makeover are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Lincolnshire looking round a pair of rural semi-detached cottages which the new owner may convert into one home. Martin Roberts is in Cheshire at a bungalow with bags of potential, and Lucy Alexander is in Kent talking to friends embarking on their first project together.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house in Chesterton in Staffordshire has a smelly problem and needs a total overhaul. The new owner of a semi in Bestwood in Nottingham also has a lot to do, while the new owner of a flat in Strawberry Hill has less than a week to sort out her auction buy before she jets off to Germany.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A husband and wife duo add their design skills to a house in Croydon in Surrey, while a seasoned developer creates a high-end house in multiple occupation in Coventry and a first time developer also does a great job with a house full of period features in Nutall in Nottinghamshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets one of two young brothers busy building a property portfolio in Bolsover, Derbyshire, while Dion Dublin checks out a three-bedroom semi in Wolverhampton, and Lucy Alexander is impressed by plans to convert a two-bedroom, fire-damaged terraced house in Londons Plaistow into a smart five-bedroom property.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin has a hair-raising experience at a barbers in Rochdale, Lancashire, while Martin Roberts looks round a great bungalow in Alsager, Cheshire, and Lucy Alexander sees all the potential in a piece of land in Eccles in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a house in Otterton, Devon, which is given a stunning transformation to match the views from the windows, Lucy Alexander looks round a small one-bedroom flat in Tottenham, London, and Dion Dublin peers into a building in Alvaston, Derby, which is in a dangerous state of disrepair.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a house in Otterton, Devon, which is given a stunning transformation to match the views from the windows, Lucy Alexander looks round a small one-bedroom flat in Tottenham, London, and Dion Dublin peers into a building in Alvaston, Derby, which is in a dangerous state of disrepair.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin visit a one-bedroom flat in Catford, London, a large house near the River Mersey and a semi-detached property in Alvaston in Derby.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An end-terrace house in Alfreton in Derby bought by two brothers, a mid-terrace on the coast at Seaham in County Durham and a large three-storey property with a flat and shop in Taylorstown in the Rhondda Valley are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin talk to the new owners to find out what they have planned for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts falls in love with a quirky cottage in Malvern bought by a builder and his wife, Dion Dublin talks to a first-time property developer who sees her end-terrace house in Chell in Stoke-on-Trent as something for her pension pot, and Lucy Alexander meets a divorced couple who have decided to form a new union as property developers doing up a shop and flat in Dover, Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large piece of land in Ratby in Leicestershire, a terrace in Chesterton in Staffordshire and a 1930s semi with a huge garden and huge potential are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the developers hoping to breathe new life into their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two-bedroom detached house in Basford in Nottingham, a top floor flat in Portsmouth and a large Edwardian terrace in Crewe are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear about their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A multimillion-pound development in Farningham in Kent, an ambitious, total renovation project in Aspatria in Cumbria and a very small studio flat in Wallington in Surrey are all sold under the hammer. Martin and Lucy meet the new owners to discuss their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A semi in Sutton Coldfield near Birmingham, a derelict house in a rural village near Dover in Kent and a basement without planning permission in Ipswich in Suffolk are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 20-year-old first-time developer, a banker turned developer and a builder who wants to create a new home for his soon-to-be-wife all buy properties at auction. Martin, Lucy and Dion are in Chesterton, Hornchurch and Sudbrook to hear their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Still a Mystery
programme.desc: Re-examine a current true crime case where unanswered questions remain and the mystery is still unraveling. Hear from those closest to the victims, including family, friends, local journalists and law enforcement.

programme.title: Til Death Do Us Part
programme.desc: The bond between twin brothers proves to be stronger than the one between a husband and wife; true love takes a tragic, deadly turn.

programme.desc: Manjit and Mukhtiar get married and set up a home together in suburbia. But fierce arguments soon turn their dreams into a nightmare.

programme.title: On the Case With Paula Zahn
programme.desc: An unidentified woman is found callously murdered in an off-campus apartment; police have no leads on the case until her terrified neighbour shares a chilling story.

programme.title: Southern Fried Homicide
programme.desc: As a kid Pat always got what she wanted. Now grown up, Pat still likes to get what she desires - anyone who dares to get in her way will soon be pushing up daisies.

programme.title: Southern Fried Homicide
programme.desc: The citizens of DeKalb County elect a new Sheriff after a history of corruption, but the ousted Sheriff has other ideas that lead to an assassination.

programme.title: Hometown Homicide
programme.desc: When excellent student Danielle Locklear disappeared, police considered her a runaway. But, her family decide to investigate themselves and the truth shocks them.

programme.title: Hometown Homicide
programme.desc: One evening in 2010, 40-year-old Rachael Anderson called a friend regarding a home improvement project. Then, she vanished and eight years later is still missing.

programme.title: American Monster
programme.desc: Shirley Merritt is devoted to her family, especially her youngest son, Richard, but he wants to have the good life by any means possible. When bad choices lead to a prison sentence, Richard makes a deadly decision, and his secret is exposed.

programme.title: American Monster
programme.desc: Ann Metz lives for her family, particularly her two beautiful granddaughters; her husband Marshall despises Anns giving her time to anyone but him, and he will go to desperate measures to keep hold of her.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates
programme.desc: When Mike Williams fails to return from a duck hunting excursion, its thought he drowned in a boating accident. However, 17 years later, his murdered body is found.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates
programme.desc: A fire chiefs wife is found burnt to death in her home. But an autopsy shocks a small Louisiana town by revealing the cause of death to be a gunshot to the head.

programme.title: Diabolical: Deadly Love
programme.desc: Tim Koile has it all: a beautiful wife and loving family. But a sordid affair with his seductive new neighbour quickly turns into something much more sinister.

programme.title: Diabolical: Deadly Love
programme.desc: Cindy McKay says she had to bend the law to pay off her late husbands debts. But when officers realise shes a criminal, can they find her before she strikes again?

programme.title: Jessica Chambers: An ID Murder Mystery
programme.desc: Explore the haunting case of Jessica Chambers, a 19-year-old who managed a dying, cryptic whisper before taking her last breath.

programme.title: A Killer Among Friends
programme.desc: A killer stabs UCLA student Ron Baker to death in a train tunnel known for its connection to the Manson murders. Occult rumours begin to spread - until another missing friend redirects suspicion to Bakers inner circle.

programme.title: Murder Under the Friday Night Lights
programme.desc: Middle School cheer captain Tristyn Bailey is the future of Friday night lights at Creekside High. Police investigating her disappearance have to navigate teenage rumours and gossip in their small Florida town to identify a monster in their midst.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here
programme.sub-title: Murder in the House of Prayer
programme.desc: As a child, Joy believes her mother, Anna Young, is the voice of God. In their isolated church community, Anna punishes anyone who breaks her rules, even young children arent spared. Years later, Joy realises her mother is not an angel.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here
programme.desc: At first, Ainjil thought William was charming and creative. Then, his violent tendencies emerged and Ainjil fled, but her ordeal was not over yet.

programme.title: Welcome To Killertown
programme.desc: When Charlie Kinkel vanishes in a remote Nevada town, locals shun his wife when she investigates. The discovery of a body reveals a case of misplaced suspicion.

programme.title: Welcome To Killertown
programme.desc: Donna Fontaine and her boyfriend are found shot dead in a burning house. Detectives face a wall of silence until a jilted lover brings evidence to light.

programme.title: Faking It: Tears of a Crime
